summary: Don't underestimate them. Light on star quality but heavy on mental fortitude — they reached the finals via back-to-back play-off penalty-shootout wins over Ireland and Denmark under Miroslav Koubek (who replaced the sacked Ivan Hašek). A dogged back five built on defensive resilience and the most set-piece-reliant attack in Europe.
style: Back five (3-4-3 / 3-4-2-1) — wing-backs high and wide, wingers tucking inside as second strikers, enormous crossing volume. Against better sides it becomes a purely defensive block: attackers drop deep and dig in, then spring forward on the counter through Pavel Šulc's ball-carrying.

counterReliance.note: Defensive block that breaks out via Šulc; the real weapon is the dead ball

strengths: Set-piece kings — 45% of qualifying goals from dead balls, the highest share of any European side
The full set-piece universe: play-off goals from a penalty, a free kick, a corner and a long throw
Clear, drilled defensive responsibilities in a back five — no confusion, unlike South Korea
Elite crossing volume (3rd highest in Europe)
weaknesses: Limited in open play — among the most big chances missed
Little star quality; reliant on resilience and dead balls rather than sustained attacking quality

keyPlayers.name: Tomáš Souček
keyPlayers.note: Wins duels, mops up, and a huge aerial threat — most headed goals of any PL midfielder since Feb 2020 (11)

keyPlayers.name: Pavel Šulc
keyPlayers.note: Pacey, direct dribbler with creativity and end product — the outlet from the block; 14 goal involvements this season

tactics: Sit in a disciplined back five, deny space, and counter. The set-piece threat is the genuine edge — defend a corner, free kick or long throw against them at your peril. Mental fortitude is the calling card after two play-off shootout wins.
notes: Hašek was sacked after a poor qualifying campaign that included a 2-1 home loss to the Faroe Islands; Koubek won his first two games — both shootouts — to reach the finals.
Koubek, 74, is among the oldest coaches at the tournament alongside South Africa's Broos.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group A (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
